items.name: White Rice
items.storageNote: In sealed Mylar bag with oxygen absorbers. Shelf life drops to 2–5 years without oxygen removal.

items.name: Hard Winter Wheat (Whole)
items.storageNote: Whole berries last far longer than flour. Requires grain mill to use.

items.name: Rolled Oats
items.storageNote: In sealed bucket with oxygen absorbers. Oats go rancid faster than rice due to fat content.

items.name: All-Purpose Flour
items.storageNote: Short shelf life. Rotate every 1–2 years. Store in sealed container away from humidity.

items.name: Dried Beans (Pinto/Black/Kidney)
items.storageNote: After 10+ years taste and texture degrade but nutritional value remains. Store in sealed Mylar.

items.name: Lentils
items.storageNote: Cook faster than beans (20–30 min). High protein. Rotate frequently.

items.name: Canned Tuna / Salmon
items.storageNote: Check for bulging or rust. Do not store in temperature extremes. Water-packed is more versatile.

items.name: Honey
items.storageNote: Indefinite shelf life if sealed and kept dry. Crystallization is normal — warm gently to reliquefy. Also has antibacterial properties.

items.name: White Sugar
items.storageNote: Indefinite shelf life in sealed, dry container. Keeps ants and moisture out.

items.name: Salt (Iodized)
items.storageNote: Indefinite shelf life. Iodized salt provides essential iodine — critical if diet lacks seafood.

items.name: Cooking Oil (Coconut / Olive)
items.storageNote: Most oils go rancid in 1–2 years. Coconut oil lasts up to 2 years; olive oil 18–24 months. Store dark and cool.

items.name: Freeze-Dried Vegetables
items.storageNote: Rehydrate with water. Keep sealed — oxygen degrades quality rapidly once opened.

items.name: Multivitamin Supplements
items.storageNote: Potency degrades over time. Rotate every 2–3 years. Critical during extended food storage diet to prevent deficiencies.

items.name: Baking Powder / Baking Soda
items.storageNote: Baking soda lasts longer than baking powder. Test potency: mix with vinegar — should bubble vigorously.

items.name: Powdered Milk (Non-fat)
items.storageNote: Non-fat powder lasts much longer than whole milk powder. Sealed with oxygen absorbers in cool, dark location.

seeds.name: Heirloom Tomato
seeds.note: Open-pollinated; save seeds from harvest each year. Grows in most climates.

seeds.name: Heirloom Squash
seeds.note: Extremely high yield. Stores well whole for months at room temperature.

seeds.name: Heirloom Beans
seeds.note: Dual-purpose: eat fresh or dry for storage. High protein.

seeds.name: Kale / Chard
seeds.note: Cold tolerant; grows in poor soil. High nutrient density per plant.

seeds.name: Heirloom Corn
seeds.note: Open-pollinated non-GMO. Can be dried and ground. Nixtamalization improves nutrition.

seeds.name: Sunflower
seeds.note: High calorie seeds and extractable oil. Birds may compete — net if needed.

seeds.name: Radish
seeds.note: Fast-growing (25 days to harvest). Excellent morale crop.

calorieTargetNote: 2,000 cal/day is a moderate activity baseline. Increase to 2,500–3,000 for heavy physical labor (bugging out, manual work).
